message: Node started: Problem Class: Minimum Spanning Tree / Steiner Tree variant on a 2D grid with directed monotonicity constraints. Objective: Minimize the total Manhattan distance cost C = sum((tx - fx) + (ty - fy)) required to connect a set of N target points (a_i, b_i) to a starting root (0, 0). Structure: A directed acyclic graph (DAG) where each node represents a beverage coordinate. Each edge (fx, fy) -> (tx, ty) must satisfy the monotonicity constraints tx >= fx and ty >= fy. State-Transition Rules: A new node can only be created if its source node already exists in the set of created nodes. The set of created nodes must eventually contain all N target points. Input Specification: An integer N followed by N pairs of coordinates (a_i, b_i). Output Specification: An integer m representing the total number of operations, followed by m lines each containing four integers: from_x, from_y, to_x, to_y. Critical Property: The solution must minimize the total Manhattan distance of the edges used to reach all target points while maintaining the coordinate monotonicity and the connectivity requirement starting from (0, 0).
